_note: Round-14 follow-up #5: a slot-attr callback body whose nested block declares `let row` shadows the outer template-local `row` only inside that block. The reference AFTER the block must still be rewritten as a template binding. Pre-fix `walk_statement_for_value_rewrite` pushed declarations onto the shadow stack but never restored at block boundaries, so the inner `let row` leaked to the outer scope and the trailing `row.id` reference was treated as still-shadowed; the rewrite was skipped and bare `row` leaked to module scope (TS2304 since module scope has no `row`). Post-fix `BlockStatement` snapshots `shadowed.len()` on entry and truncates on exit (mirrored across `For`/`ForIn`/`ForOf`/`While`/`DoWhile`/`Switch`/`Try`-block/`Try`-finalizer for completeness); the trailing reference rewrites correctly to `(undefined as any as (Row)).id` and Producer type-checks clean. Consumer's `let:doubled` resolves to `number[]`; `takesNumberArray(doubled)` passes.
